displayTitle: Lead Screens: Faster Film, Cleaner Image

beats.narration: Industrial X ray film captures only about one percent of the radiation that reaches it. Lead screens exist to claw that wasted energy back.

payload.title: THE LEAD SCREEN
payload.sub: Thin foil that speeds the exposure and cleans the image.

beats.narration: Three jobs: photoelectrons expose the film at contact range, low-energy scatter gets absorbed, and the thicker back screen stops backscatter.
Intensifying factor: exposure without screens divided by exposure with screens. Lead gives two to four. Fluorescent? Ten to one hundred, but spreading light costs sharpness.
Screens must touch the film everywhere; gaps blur. Keep them clean, too: trapped lint prints a light mark that can mimic a flaw.
The rule: clean, flat, in contact. Anything between screen and film prints, and one fuzzy patch can hide a fine crack.

payload.sub: Anything between screen and film prints.
